steps.m: to line up or arrange in rows

steps.n: Added vowels and doubled the final letter to show basic past tense form
Added alif after first letter to show the doer of the action
Added feminine plural ending to show multiple beings who do this action
Added kasra ending to show this word is connected to what follows
Added conjunction و to connect this with what follows

steps.m: and the ones who line up (possessed/connected)
steps.n: Added 'the' (ال) at the start and made the vowel longer to emphasize the meaning

steps.m: to line up or arrange in order

steps.n: The doubled 'f' sound shows this is now a noun meaning 'a row'

steps.m: a row (object of الصَّآفَّاتِ - those who arrange)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this is what is being arranged by الصَّآفَّاتِ (those who arrange in rows)
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Changed pattern to show who does the action
Added feminine plural ending

steps.m: the female ones who drive away
steps.n: Added definite article and doubled the ز because it comes after ال

steps.m: and the female ones who drive away (subject of زَجْرًا)
steps.n: Added connecting فَ at start, changed alif to alif wasl (ٱ), and added kasra at end because it's part of a series. This word is the subject doing the action of زَجْرًا (driving away) that follows it.
Changed from basic root meaning 'drive away' into noun meaning 'driving away' by adding marks (fatha-sukun pattern)
Added tanwin alif to show this is the object being acted upon by الزَّاجِرٰتِ (those who drive away). This emphasizes the action itself.
Changes from verb 'to follow' to 'one who follows'
Adding ات makes it feminine plural. This word is the subject (doer) of the recitation mentioned in ذِكْرًا.
Adding ال makes it definite (like 'the' in English)

steps.m: And the female ones who follow/recite
steps.n: Adding فَ (and) as a conjunction

steps.m: And the female ones who follow/recite
steps.n: Adding kasra at the end to show it's connected to previous word
Changed the action into a thing by adding ِ under first letter and ْ on middle letter. Like how 'remember' becomes 'remembrance' in English.

steps.m: a remembrance/mention (object of التَّالِيٰتِ - those who recite)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this is what's being recited. The double marks also show it's any remembrance, not a specific one. This is what the reciters (التَّالِيٰتِ) are reciting.
This particle makes what comes after it stronger and more certain. Here it emphasizes that 'your god is one.' The shadda (ّ) makes the emphasis even stronger.
Changes from a verb about worshipping into a noun meaning the thing being worshipped (a god)
The long 'aa' sound is written with a small alif above instead of a regular alif
Adds fatha because it's the object of the particle إِنَّ which comes before it
Adds 2nd person masculine plural possessive pronoun suffix
Root letters connected with vowels to make the basic word for 'one'
Added alif after first letter to make it mean 'one' as a noun/adjective
Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, showing this is definitely true
The word got a double-dammah (ٌ) at the end to show it completes the meaning of إِنَّ (indeed). Also, the alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) on top.
Doubling the ب shows intensity of nurturing/lordship
The ُ shows this word is the subject of the sentence
Adds اء to make it a noun referring to something high up
Added وات to make it plural, showing there are multiple heavens
Added ال to make it specific/definite, like saying 'the' in English
Added ِ to show it belongs to رَبُّ (Lord/Master). The alif is written as ٰ in this style.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make it a noun meaning the physical earth
وَ means 'and', ٱلْ makes it specific ('the earth'), and ِ shows it's affected by فِى
This is a particle that refers to things in general. In this ayah, it specifically refers to what exists between the heavens and earth.
وَ adds 'and' to connect this with the previous phrase about the heavens and earth

steps.m: to separate or be distinct from

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to separate' into noun meaning 'between'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is connecting things together (like saying 'between X and Y')

steps.m: between them both (referring to السَّمٰوٰتِ and ٱلْأَرْضِ)
steps.n: Added هُمَا to show this refers to exactly two things (the heavens and earth mentioned in the ayah)
The doubled middle letter shows intensity, making it mean 'one who completely nurtures/sustains'
The ُ (damma) shows this word is doing something in the sentence. Here it's the subject that owns/controls what comes after it (the Easts)

steps.m: and the Lord (subject of the sentence)
steps.n: Adding وَ (and) connects this to the previous phrase about being Lord of the heavens and earth
Added مَ at the start to show where something happens, like how 'mosque' is where praying happens
Added ا to make it refer to multiple places where the sun rises

steps.m: the places of sunrise/the eastern points
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific eastern points

steps.m: of the eastern points (possessed by رَبُّ - Lord)
steps.n: Changed to show it belongs to 'Lord' (رَبُّ) - like saying 'eastern points of the Lord'
This is a particle that adds emphasis to what comes after it. It makes the statement stronger, like adding 'definitely' in English.

steps.m: indeed we (subject of زَيَّنَّا - we adorned)
steps.n: Added ا at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. This connects to the next word زَيَّنَّا (we adorned) where 'we' are doing the adorning.
Adding vowel marks makes this a complete past tense verb meaning 'he decorated'
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action, and doubled the middle letter ي to show intensity. This word follows إِنَّا (indeed we) in the verse.

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated

steps.n: Added vowels to make basic past tense verb
Adds ء at the end to change from 'rising up' (action) to 'sky' (thing)
The word gets 'the' (ال) in front, which doubles the first letter س to make 'the sky'

steps.m: the sky/heaven (object of زَيَّنَّا - we adorned)
steps.n: The word gets an 'a' sound at the end to show it's receiving the action of being adorned
Root د-ن-و means 'near' - added vowels and ي to make the word 'worldly' or 'this world'
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the د letter because it comes after ال
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Forms a concrete noun meaning 'decoration' from the root meaning 'to beautify'

steps.m: with decoration (describes how اللَّهُ decorated السَّمَآءَ)
steps.n: The word shows how Allah decorated the sky, using بِ to mean 'with' and tanwin (ٍ) to show it's indefinite (any decoration)
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun
Changes single star/planet into multiple stars/planets by adding alif
Added ٱلْ to make it definite and ِ to show it describes how the sky is decorated
Changed from verb (to guard) into noun (guarding/protection) by adding ِ under first letter and ْ on middle letter
Added ًا at the end to show this word is being used as an object in the sentence, like saying 'We made it a protection'

steps.m: and a protection (being used as object)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at beginning to connect this with the previous phrase
Basic preposition showing source or origin
The م gets a shadda (doubled) because it comes after the word وَحِفْظًا which ends with a similar sound. This is just for easier pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.
Added damma and doubled the middle letter to create a word meaning 'all' or 'every'
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to شَيْطٰنٍ (devil) that comes after it, showing it means 'every devil' or 'all devils'
The ي makes the word stronger, showing something that happens a lot
The added ا makes the meaning even stronger

steps.m: any extremely rebellious one (being protected against)
steps.n: The tanwin (ٍ) shows this is indefinite (any devil) and is being acted upon in relation to حِفْظًا (protection) mentioned earlier in the ayah
Changes from verb (to rebel) to noun (one who rebels)

steps.m: a very rebellious one (owned by/connected to something)
steps.n: Added shadda (double letter) at start and two dots under to show it's describing something else (the word 'shaytan')
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَسَّمَّعُونَ (they listen) into 'they do not listen'
The shadda (ّ) and fatha (َ) make the negation stronger, showing that they definitely cannot listen to the highest assembly
These are the basic letters that give us the core meaning of hearing or listening
Adding vowels (a-a-a) to make it 'he heard' in the past tense
Adding يَ at the start and ُ at the end makes it present tense
Doubling the س and م letters shows doing the action with extra effort

steps.m: they try very hard to hear (to the highest assembly)
steps.n: Adding ون shows that many people are doing this action. In this ayah, they are trying to hear what's being said in the highest assembly (ٱلْمَلَإِ ٱلْأَعْلَىٰ)
This is a basic directional particle that shows movement or direction towards something. In this ayah, it connects 'they cannot listen' with 'the highest assembly'
Changed the word into a noun meaning the result of gathering - an assembly or group
Added the definite article ال and kasra at the end because this word is connected to 'the highest' (ٱلْأَعْلَىٰ) in the ayah
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added أ at beginning and changed vowels to make it mean 'higher/highest'
Added ال to make it 'the highest' instead of just 'highest'
The alif at the end is just written differently - same word and meaning, referring to the highest of the chiefs/assembly
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Changes to passive voice present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Changes to plural by adding ون, showing multiple people are being thrown
Adds و at beginning to connect this action with the previous statement
Basic particle that shows direction or source. Here it connects with جَانِبٍ (side) to show 'from every side'

steps.m: to be complete or surround completely
steps.n: Becomes a noun meaning 'all' or 'every'
Added damma and shadda to create the noun meaning 'all/every'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to جَانِبٍ (side) that comes after it, making it mean 'of every side'
Connected the root letters together to form the base word

steps.m: one who goes to the side
steps.n: Changed to active participle form to show 'one who does the action'

steps.m: from any side (object of مِن كُلِّ)
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra to show this is the object of the phrase 'from every/all' (مِن كُلِّ)

steps.m: to push away forcefully or repel

steps.n: Changed the word pattern to show intense or repeated action of driving away

steps.m: with forceful repelling (describing how they are driven away)
steps.n: Added an -an ending to show it describes how they are driven away (like saying 'forcefully')
Starting with the basic preposition that shows direction or purpose
Added و to connect this phrase to what came before it (عَذَابٌ وَاصِبٌ)

steps.m: and for them (connected to عَذَابٌ - punishment)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show this is happening to them. This connects to عَذَابٌ (punishment) in the ayah, showing they will receive the punishment.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: punishment (noun from the act of punishing)
steps.n: Adding an alif between the second and third root letters turns this into a noun that describes the action. This word is in the nominative case as it appears as a standalone subject.
Adding tanween dammah (ٌ) makes this an indefinite noun. This word is the subject of وَاصِبٌ (lasting/constant) in this ayah.

steps.m: to persist or continue without stopping

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changed to active participle pattern to show something that keeps doing the action. This word describes عَذَابٌ (punishment) as being perpetual/never-ending.
Adding double dammah makes it indefinite - 'a perpetual (punishment)'
This is a basic word that shows an exception. In this ayah, it connects to the words that follow (مَنْ خَطِفَ) to say that those who snatch something are an exception to what was mentioned before.
This is a special word that means 'whoever' or 'one who'. In this ayah, it refers to the one who does the action of خَطِفَ (snatches). It's like saying 'except whoever snatches...'
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb
Changed to show one instance of snatching
Added 'the' at beginning and made it the object of the verb 'snatched' from earlier in the verse
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: he made (something) follow/he sent in pursuit
steps.n: Added أ at the beginning and changed vowels to show making someone else do the action

steps.m: and so he sent in pursuit
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happened after something else (in this case, after someone snatched something as mentioned in the previous part of the verse)

steps.m: and so he pursued him (referring to whoever snatched something in the previous part of the verse)
steps.n: Added هُ (hu) at the end to show who was being chased/followed
Added vowels and the long 'aa' sound (ا) to make it into a noun meaning the thing that flames/burns

steps.m: a piercing flame (describing the word ثَاقِبٌ)
steps.n: Added tanween (double damma) to show this is indefinite (like saying 'a' flame). This word is being described by ثَاقِبٌ (piercing) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed the word pattern to show who/what does the action. This describes the flame/meteor (شِهَابٌ) as something that pierces through.
Added tanwin damma (ٌ) to show this is describing an undefined thing
Adding 'است' changes the meaning from giving to seeking an opinion
Adding فَ shows this action follows from something before it. The ٱ is added for pronunciation.

steps.m: so ask them (for their opinion) (connected to أَهُمْ أَشَدُّ)
steps.n: Adding هِمْ makes it 'ask them.' This connects to the next phrase 'Are they stronger in creation?' showing what to ask them about.
Added diacritics to show proper pronunciation - damma (ُ) shows it's the subject form of 'they' and sukun (ْ) marks the end
Added أَ at the beginning to turn this into a question. This is asking if 'they' are stronger, referring to the disbelievers being asked about in this verse.
Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
Forms comparative meaning by adding أَ at start and changing final vowel. This word is being compared to خَلْقًا (creation) in this ayah.

steps.m: to create, form, or shape something

steps.n: Connected the root letters and added fathas to show this happened in the past
Changed from a verb (created) to a noun (creation) by putting a sukun on the middle letter. This word is being compared to something else in the verse.
Added tanween alif to show this is the thing being compared when asking 'Are they stronger in creation?'

steps.m: or (connecting to أَهُمْ in the previous part)
steps.n: This is a special word used in questions to present alternatives, like asking 'Are they...or...?' Here it connects back to أَهُمْ (are they) to ask if one thing or another is true.
This is the basic interrogative pronoun used to ask about people
The shadda shows this word joins smoothly with the word before it when reading. It asks 'who?' and connects to 'we created' after it.

steps.m: to make something new from nothing

steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha marks to show this is something that happened in the past
Changed the last vowel to a stop mark (sukun)
Added نَآ at the end to show 'we' did the action
This is an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows
Added نا (we) at the end to show who is speaking. This word connects to خَلَقْنٰهُم (we created them) in this ayah.
Added fathas to connect the letters and make it past tense
Adding نا changes it from third person (he) to first person plural (we)
Added هُم to show who was created (them). The alif in نا becomes ٰ (dagger alif) when connected to هُم
Fundamental preposition indicating origin, source, or starting point
Added shadda to show this word connects strongly to the next word طِينٍ (clay). Together they mean 'from clay'
Connected the letters and added kasra to make it into a noun
Added tanween to show this is clay that something is being done to or with (in this case, humans being created from it)
Added vowels and alif to make an active participle (something that does the sticking). This describes the clay mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Added shadda for emphasis since this clay is being described as particularly sticky
Added tanwin kasra to show this is describing an indefinite noun (the clay) in the genitive case

steps.m: Rather (contradicting what came before and introducing عَجِبْتَ 'you were amazed')
steps.n: This is a particle that doesn't come from a root. It's used to switch from one topic to another or to correct a previous statement. Here it introduces a contrast with what came before, leading into 'you were amazed'.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense form. This word is connected to يَسْخَرُونَ (they mock) in this ayah.
Adding تَ changes it from 'he' to 'you'. This shows Allah addressing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ directly.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changes from past tense (he mocked) to present tense (he mocks) by adding يَ at the start
Adding ونَ makes it plural (they) instead of singular (he)

steps.m: and they mock (after عَجِبْتَ - you were amazed)
steps.n: The و connects this action to the previous phrase 'you were amazed'

steps.m: when (introducing what happens next in ذُكِّرُوا۟)
steps.n: This is a word that means 'when' and introduces what happens at a specific time. Here it introduces what these people do when they are reminded (ذُكِّرُوا۟)

steps.m: and when (introducing what happens next in ذُكِّرُوا۟)
steps.n: The و connects this new time condition to what was mentioned before, like saying 'and also when this happens...'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to passive voice with damma (ُ) on first letter, kasra (ِ) on middle letter, and shadda (ّ) doubling the middle letter to show intensity
Added وا at the end to show that many people were reminded
This is a negative particle that negates the verb يَذْكُرُونَ (they remember) that comes after it, making the meaning 'they do not remember'
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, sukun on first root letter, and damma on middle root letter
Added ون to show this is about a group of people
This is a time word that introduces a condition - it shows something happens whenever another thing happens. Here it introduces what happens when they see a sign.

steps.m: and when (introduces the condition رَأَوْا 'they see')
steps.n: The و connects this condition with what came before it, showing these events happen in sequence
The basic meaning of seeing or perceiving
Added vowels (fatha) to make the root letters into the past tense verb meaning 'he saw'
Added وا at the end to show that a group of people did the action. This word is connected to يَسْتَسْخِرُونَ (they mock) in the ayah - it tells us what they saw before mocking.
The root letters combine with the first letter becoming hamza and the double ي becoming alif
The ة transforms the root into a concrete noun meaning 'sign' or 'miracle'

steps.m: a sign/miracle (object of رَأَوْا - they saw)
steps.n: Adding the -an ending shows this is receiving the action (they saw a sign)

steps.m: to mock or make fun of
steps.n: The basic meaning of mocking or making fun of something
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb showing someone did the action of mocking
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added است to show seeking or trying to do the action
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
The middle letter و changes to ا when we add the past tense pattern. This is a common change with middle-weak verbs.
Adding وا at the end shows that many people (all male or mixed group) did the action
Added و at the beginning to connect this with what came before it
This is a negation particle that works with إِلَّا (except/but) later in the sentence to say something is not true. Here it's saying 'this is not... except magic' meaning 'this is nothing but magic'
This is the basic form of the demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby
Added ها at the beginning (with the alif shortened to just ه) to make the word stronger and clearer when pointing to something
Added fatha and lengthened the final alif to add even more emphasis, which is common in Quranic style when something important is being pointed out

This is a basic particle that shows something is being excluded or is the only option. It's like saying 'but only' or 'except for' in English. In this ayah, it's used to say that what came before (هٰذَآ - this) is nothing except what comes after (سِحْرٌ مُّبِينٌ - clear magic).

steps.m: except, only (connecting هٰذَآ to سِحْرٌ مُّبِينٌ)
steps.n: The shadda and alif are added to complete the particle's standard form. This emphasizes the exclusion, making it stronger - like saying 'absolutely nothing except' or 'only and nothing else'.
Changed the spelling action into a noun for the magical power itself. This word is what's being called 'this' (هٰذَآ) in the verse.
Added two small marks (tanween) at the end to show this is a complete subject word
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Adding ٌ at the end makes it indefinite (not talking about a specific one)
Adding مُ makes it mean 'one who does the action'
This is the basic word meaning 'when' that indicates a future condition

steps.m: is it when? (expressing disbelief about مِتْنَا - our death)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning to make it a question, with an extra hamza (ء) to show strong disbelief or denial. This is asking in disbelief about being resurrected after death.
Added vowels (a) to make the root 'die' into 'he died' in past tense
Changed vowels to make the verb 'he died' into the noun 'death'
Changed مِوْتَ to مِتْ and added نَا at the end to mean 'we died'

steps.m: to exist or be in a state

steps.n: Changed root letters into a command by adding damma (u) and sukun (stop mark) - means 'Be!'
Added shadda and fatha to make it mean 'they (women) were' instead of 'be!'
Added ا (alif) at the end to change from 'they (feminine)' to 'we' - this ا shows the speaker is included
Added و with fatha to connect this word to what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changes verb 'became dust' into noun meaning 'dust' itself

steps.m: dust (as the thing being acted upon)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha to show this is what's being acted upon in the sentence - they are asking if they will be brought back to life after becoming dust
Changed the root into a noun for a single bone
Changed from single bone to multiple bones using broken plural pattern
Added tanween alif to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence
Added و at the beginning to connect this word with the previous word تُرَابًا (dust)
This is the basic emphatic particle that adds certainty to a statement
Added نا at the end to show this refers to 'we'

steps.m: is it true that we...? (expressing disbelief about لَمَبْعُوثُونَ - being resurrected)
steps.n: Added أَءِ at the beginning to turn this into a question expressing surprise or disbelief. This question is about being resurrected (لَمَبْعُوثُونَ) which comes later in the ayah.
Changed vowels to show this happened to someone rather than someone doing it
Changes from verb (was raised) to noun (one who is raised)
Changes from singular (one who is raised) to plural (many who are raised)
Adds لَ to emphasize that they will definitely be resurrected
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic noun
Changed to broken plural form for 'fathers/ancestors'
Added 'our' suffix. The ء changes to ؤ before نا due to Arabic spelling rules
Added damma to show this is the subject of the sentence
Added أَ to turn the statement into a question
Doubling the middle letter makes it mean 'definitely first'
Added damma to make it refer to a single person or thing that is first
Added ون to make it plural, referring to multiple people or things that are first
Added ال (with proper spelling as ٱلْ) to specify these particular first ones
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense form

Changed to a command form. The middle و is dropped because it's a hollow verb (has و in middle). This is a command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak to the disbelievers, as shown by the next ayah which contains their response.
This is a particle used to strongly agree or say 'yes' in response to a question or statement. Here it's answering the command قُلْ (say).
This is a pronoun that means 'you' when talking to multiple people. In this ayah, it's connected to دٰخِرُونَ (humbled/disgraced) to say 'you all are humbled'
The و at the beginning connects this to what came before (نَعَمْ - 'yes'), making it 'yes, and you all...'

steps.m: to be humbled, to be lowly

steps.n: Changed to describe someone who is in a state of being humbled. This word describes the state of 'you all' (أنتم) in this ayah.
Made plural with ون to match with أنتم (you all). The ُ shows this is the main part that tells us what 'you all' are.
This is the basic emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from what came before
Added مَا at the end which combines with إِنَّ to create the meaning of 'only' or 'just'
This is a basic pronoun used to refer to feminine singular things or people. In this ayah, it refers to the 'blast' (زَجْرَةٌ) that will happen on the Day of Judgment.
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters together. The fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter make this into a basic past-tense verb.

steps.m: a single shout/cry (that will resurrect everyone)
steps.n: The ة shows this is one specific instance of shouting. The تنوين (ٌ) shows it's indefinite (like 'a' in English). This refers to the single shout that will resurrect everyone on the Day of Judgment.
Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he became one'
Changes from verb 'became one' to noun meaning 'one who is single/united'
Added ة to make it feminine, and the alif changed to a long alif (ٰ)
Added tanwin (ٌ) to show this is indefinite (like 'a' or 'an' in English)
This is a particle that shows something happening unexpectedly or draws attention to what follows

steps.m: and then suddenly (followed by هُمْ يَنظُرُونَ - they will be looking)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement and show sequence. This introduces what happens next in the story.
This is a basic pronoun used to refer to three or more masculine things or people. In this ayah, it's the subject of the verb يَنظُرُونَ (they look/observe).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
The يَ at the start shows it's happening now or in the future
The ون ending shows this is for three or more people. This word relates to هُمْ (they) in the ayah.
The root letters ق-و-ل become قَالَ (he said) by changing و to ا - this is a common change in past tense verbs with و in the middle
Adding وا at the end makes it refer to a group (they) instead of just one person (he)
Adding وَ at the beginning connects this word to what came before it. This word is followed by يٰوَيْلَنَا (Oh, woe to us!) showing what they said.
This is the base word meaning misfortune or destruction. It's often used as an exclamation of distress.
Added يٰ at the beginning to show someone is calling out in distress

steps.m: Oh, woe to us! (exclamation of those speaking in وَقَالُوا۟)

steps.m: the one who/which/that (connects يَوْمُ with كُنتُم)

This is a common particle in Arabic that adds emphasis, especially when talking about things that happened in the past. It's like adding 'definitely' or 'certainly' in English.
Adding لَ makes the emphasis even stronger. This extra emphasis shows how certain it is that Allah sent the warners.

steps.m: and indeed definitely (emphasizes أَرْسَلْنَا - we sent)
steps.n: وَ connects this phrase to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the start makes the verb causative, showing that someone is making the action happen. The sukun (ْ) is added on ر for pronunciation.
Adding نَا changes the subject to 'we' and shows past tense. This verb connects to مُّنذِرِينَ (warners) which tells who was sent.
This is a basic preposition meaning 'in' or 'within'

steps.m: in them (referring to those who received warners)
steps.n: Added هِم which shows this applies to a group of people (them). This connects to the previous context about sending warners to people.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb

steps.m: and look!/so observe! (connected to what comes after: كَيْفَ كَانَ عٰقِبَةُ ٱلْمُنذَرِينَ)

steps.m: how (asking about the state/manner of عٰقِبَةُ - end result)

Adding the basic vowel marks to show how to pronounce the word correctly

steps.m: he/it was (describes what happened to عٰقِبَةُ - the end result)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels in a past tense verb. This is a common change in Arabic that makes the word easier to say.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

Added damma to show this is the subject of كَانَ (was). The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) but pronounced the same.

steps.m: to warn or alert of danger

steps.n: Past tense verb meaning 'he warned' - basic 3 letter past tense pattern with fatha marks
Added مُ (mu) at start and changed vowels to make it mean 'the one being warned' instead of 'he warned'

steps.m: except (introduces عِبَادَ ٱللَّهِ as an exception)
steps.n: This is a common exception particle that introduces something excluded from what came before. In this ayah, it introduces 'the servants of Allah' as those who are excepted.
Added fatha vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
This makes it a broken plural showing multiple servants/worshippers
Added a fatha mark at the end because it's connected to 'Allah' to show 'servants of Allah'

Added kasra at the end to show possession/connection to المُخْلَصِينَ (the chosen ones). Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to hamzat wasl.

steps.m: one who is chosen/purified (connected to عِبَادَ - servants)

Changes from singular to plural form for multiple chosen servants

Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation
Added لَ at the beginning to add extra emphasis
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it

steps.m: to call out or cry out to someone

Added نَا at the end to show who was called out to
Added damma (ُ) to show this is the subject doing the action of calling in نَادَىٰنَا (he called us)
Added tanween (double damma ٌ) which is a common ending for proper names in Arabic
This root relates to goodness and pleasure
Changes to a special verb used to praise something

This root is about answering or responding to something

Added ون to show multiple people who respond

Added shadda to show intensive saving/rescuing, and added vowel marks to connect letters
Added نا to show 'we' did the action

Added و to connect this with the previous statement

steps.m: to belong to a family, to settle

Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb نَجَّيْنٰهُ (We saved)

steps.m: his family (object of نَجَّيْنٰهُ - We saved)
steps.n: Added pronoun هُ meaning 'his' to show possession

steps.m: and his family (object of نَجَّيْنٰهُ - We saved)

This is a preposition that shows something coming from or being separated from something else. In this ayah, it connects to 'the distress' (ٱلْكَرْبِ) to show what they were saved from.
Added a fatha at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word ٱلْكَرْبِ (the distress)

steps.m: to feel or cause intense distress/worry

steps.n: Changed from action to noun by adding vowel marks. This makes it name the feeling itself rather than the action of feeling distressed.

steps.n: Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word مِنَ (from). This word is also described by ٱلْعَظِيمِ (the great) which comes after it.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show basic past tense form
Changed pattern to create adjective showing permanent quality
Added ال to make it specific

steps.m: the Great One (describing the distress/trial mentioned before)
steps.n: Added kasra to show this describes the distress (الكرب) mentioned in the previous part of the verse

steps.m: to make something into something else

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense form showing someone made or established something
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This word is connected to ذُرِّيَّتَهُۥ (his offspring) which is what was made/established.
Added وَ at the start to mean 'and'

steps.m: to spread out, scatter (like seeds or descendants)

steps.n: Doubled the middle letter (shown by shadda) to make it mean 'a scattered thing' instead of 'to scatter'

This is a basic pronoun meaning 'they' - it doesn't come from a root. In this ayah, it's the subject that ٱلْبَاقِينَ (the remaining ones) describes.
Added damma marks (ُ) to show this is the subject form of 'they'
Added alif after first root letter and vowels to show who does the action. This word tells us more about 'they' (هُم) in the sentence.
Added ينَ at the end to show we're talking about many people staying
Added ٱلْ at the start to point to specific people, like saying 'the' in English
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Adding نَا changes the doer from 'he' to 'we' and shows we did this action in the past

steps.m: and we left/abandoned (connected to عَلَيْهِ - upon him)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this action to what came before it. This verb connects to عَلَيْهِ (upon him) in the next part of the verse.
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or upon something else

steps.m: upon him (connected to وَتَرَكْنَا - and We left)
steps.n: Added هِ which means 'him', with ي helping connect it to the word. This shows who is being affected by the action وَتَرَكْنَا (and We left).
This is a preposition that shows something is inside, within, or among something else. In this ayah, it connects 'upon him' (عَلَيْهِ) with 'the later generations' (ٱلْءَاخِرِينَ)
The ي is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to come after or be delayed

steps.n: Adding آ at start makes it mean 'the last one'
Added ين to make it plural masculine
Added ٱلْ to make it specific. This word follows في (among) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic noun
Adding alif makes it a noun meaning a state of peace
Adding tanween makes it an indefinite noun meaning 'a peace'
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of, over, or directed towards something else. In this ayah, it connects سَلٰمٌ (peace) with نُوحٍ (Nuh).
The alif at the end is written with a special mark (ىٰ) to show it's a long 'aa' sound. This doesn't change the meaning.
This is the basic form of the prophet's name
Added tanwin kasra (ٍ) at the end because the word follows عَلَىٰ (upon/to) in سَلٰمٌ عَلَىٰ (peace be upon)
This is a preposition that shows Noah is being honored among all the worlds/peoples. It connects the word 'Noah' to 'the worlds'.
The ي is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Forms a noun meaning 'that which is known' or 'world' - everything that can be known or experienced
Forms the regular masculine plural to show multiple worlds
Adding 'al' makes it specific - referring to all the known worlds. The alif after ع is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this word.
This is a special word that makes what comes after it stronger or more certain, like adding 'definitely' in English
Added ا at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. This connects to the verb نَجْزِى (we reward) that comes later in the ayah.
This is a demonstrative pronoun that points to something far away. The كَ at the end makes it masculine.

steps.m: like that/in that way (describing how Allah rewards نَجْزِى the good-doers)
steps.n: Adding كَ at the beginning changes it from just pointing to something to comparing something to it. Here it describes how Allah rewards the good-doers.

Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show past tense meaning

steps.m: we reward (the doers of good المحسنين)
steps.n: Added نَ at start to show 'we' are doing the action. Changed vowels to show present tense. The ى at the end is because it's a weak letter at the end of a word.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this is a past tense verb
The مُ prefix creates an active participle - someone who performs the action
The ين ending makes it plural (more than two)

steps.m: the doers of good (plural, object of نَجْزِى)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and a final fatha because it's the object of the verb نَجْزِى (we reward)

steps.m: indeed he (referring to نُوحٌ mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Adds هُ which refers back to Noah (نُوحٌ) who was mentioned earlier in the story

steps.m: from/of (connects عِبَادِنَا to what comes before it)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition showing that something comes from or belongs to something else. Here it shows that someone is 'from/of' our servants (عِبَادِنَا).

steps.m: from/of (connects عِبَادِنَا to what comes before it)

Connected the root letters with fatha to create past tense verb
Changes from verb (to worship) to plural noun (worshippers)

Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

Added ال at start to mean 'the' and َ at end because this word describes 'our servants' (عِبَادِنَا) in the ayah

steps.m: then (connects this event to أَغْرَقْنَا - We drowned)
steps.n: Added the vowel marks ُ (damma) and ّ (shadda) to show how it's pronounced. This word connects to the next action in the ayah (أَغْرَقْنَا - We drowned), showing this drowning happened after something else.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

This is the basic form of the word that introduces a hypothetical situation that didn't happen in the past. In this ayah, it introduces what people wished they had (a reminder from the earlier people).
Adding the vowel marks shows this is specifically about something that didn't happen in the past. Here it introduces what people wished they had, but didn't actually have.
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation

steps.m: that (emphasizing what follows is definitely true)
steps.n: Added shadda to make the meaning stronger. This word connects to عِندَنَا (we have) that comes after it, emphasizing that what follows is definitely true
Added the vowels to make it a preposition showing location or possession
Added نَا at the end to show this is 'with us'. In this ayah, it's part of a conditional phrase starting with لَوْ أَنَّ (if we had).
Changed the root into a noun by adding the vowel marks ِ and ْ. This turns the action of remembering into the concept of remembrance.

steps.m: a remembrance/reminder (object of عِندَنَا - we have)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this is something being possessed or acted upon. This word is what 'we have' (عِندَنَا) in the verse.
Basic preposition showing source, origin, or belonging
The shadda emphasizes the م to show strong connection with previous word لَوْ (if)
The fatha at the end connects this word to the next word ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (the former people), showing it means 'from the former people'
Doubling the middle letter makes it mean 'the one that comes first'
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about many first ones or ancestors

steps.m: the first ones, the ancestors (after مِنَ meaning 'from/of')
steps.n: Added ال at the beginning to mean 'the' and َ at the end because it comes after the word مِنَ (from/of)
Added basic past tense vowels to show completed action
Added نا to show 'we' and changed vowels because it's a hollow verb (has و in middle)
Added لَ at start to add emphasis, like saying 'surely' or 'definitely'
Changes from singular to broken plural form meaning 'group of servants'
Added fatha at end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb لَكُنَّا (we would have been)
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'

This is the unique name for the one true God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the shadda emphasizes it, and the kasra at the end shows it belongs to or connects with something else in the sentence.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show someone who received purification (not the one who purified)
Changed last vowel to kasra to prepare for plural ending
Added ين to make it plural, showing it refers to multiple people

steps.m: the purified ones (who are عِبَادَ ٱللَّهِ - servants of Allah)
steps.n: Added ال to show these are specific purified ones, referring to the servants of Allah mentioned in the previous part of the verse

steps.m: to reject or deny something by covering it up

steps.n: Added fathas to make it a past tense verb showing someone did this action
Added وا to show a group of people did this action

steps.m: so they rejected/denied (it) (referring to بِهِۦ in next ayah)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happened as a result of something before. The 'it' refers to what is mentioned in بِهِۦ in the next part of the ayah.
Added kasra to show this is a preposition meaning 'with' or 'by'
Added the pronoun هِ (him/it) to show who/what is being referred to
Added a small alif (ۦ) after هِ to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
سوف is a particle that indicates something will happen in the future. Here it comes before يَعْلَمُونَ (they know) to show they will know in the future.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens after something else. Here it shows they will know as a result of what happened in the previous ayah (فَكَفَرُوا۟ بِهِۦ - they disbelieved in it).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show present tense
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
This is a common particle used to emphasize that something definitely happened in the past. In this ayah, it emphasizes the certainty of Allah's word having preceded for His messengers.
The لَ adds extra emphasis, making the certainty even stronger. This double emphasis shows how absolutely certain Allah's promise to His messengers is.
The وَ connects this phrase to what came before it, showing this is part of a continuing discussion about Allah's messengers
The basic meaning is about something coming before something else
Basic past tense form of the verb. This word is describing what our word (كَلِمَتُنَا) did.
The ت shows this is about a feminine subject (كَلِمَتُنَا/our word)
The basic meaning relates to speech or words
Added vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changes from the action of speaking to the thing that is spoken (a word)
Adds damma to show this word is doing the action in the sentence
Adds 'our' to show the word belongs to us. This word is doing the action of preceding (سَبَقَتْ) in this verse.
The basic meaning is about serving or worshipping
Root letters got vowels to make a simple noun meaning 'one who worships'
Changes to broken plural form to mean multiple servants/worshippers
Adds 'our' to show these are Allah's servants
Adds preposition meaning 'for/to' at the beginning
The basic meaning is about sending something or someone

steps.m: one who is sent as a messenger
steps.n: Adds مُ at the start to show someone receiving the action, making it mean 'one who is sent'
Makes it plural by adding ين. This shows it's describing 'our servants' (عِبَادِنَا) in this ayah
Adds ال ('the') at the start to make it specific. The ending َ shows it describes 'our servants' in the sentence
This is a particle that adds emphasis to a statement

steps.m: indeed they (emphasizes لَهُمُ ٱلْمَنصُورُونَ - they are the victorious ones)
steps.n: Adds هُمْ (they) to show who is being emphasized. This connects to the next phrase لَهُمُ ٱلْمَنصُورُونَ (they are the victorious ones), emphasizing that THEY specifically will be victorious.
This is a preposition showing who will receive the help mentioned in المَنصُورُونَ (the helped/victorious ones)

steps.m: for them/to them (the ones who will be المَنصُورُونَ)
steps.n: Adds هُمُ to show this refers to a group of people (mentioned in the previous word إِنَّهُمْ) who will receive the help/victory
This is the basic meaning of the root letters
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to show who receives the help rather than who gives it
Added ون to show we're talking about multiple people
Added ال to make it specific and added َ at the end to show it's part of a complete sentence
This is a basic word that adds emphasis to what comes after it, like adding 'definitely' or 'surely' in English

steps.m: and indeed (connects to previous statement while emphasizing what follows)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this statement with what came before it, like saying 'and' in English

steps.m: to gather or assemble (like an army)

steps.n: Added ُ and ْ to make the noun meaning 'army' from the idea of gathering
Added a fatha (َ) at the end because 'inna' (indeed) came before and makes this word the object
Added نَا at the end to show this is our army. This word is being affected by إِنَّ (indeed) which comes before it in the verse.
Basic preposition showing direction or purpose. This connects to الْغٰلِبُونَ (the victorious ones) to show who will be victorious.

steps.m: for them (the ones who are الْغٰلِبُونَ)
steps.n: Added هُمُ which means 'them' (for a group of males or mixed group). The مُ at the end helps connect this word to the next word الْغٰلِبُونَ (the victorious ones).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes from past tense verb (he overcame) to a noun meaning 'one who does the overcoming'
Adds -oon ending to make it mean 'many people who overcome' instead of just one person
Adding ال makes it definite (like 'the' in English)
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
The ي changed to a doubled ل and ى to show someone turning away strongly or completely
Adding تَ shows doing the action to oneself
Added فَ to show sequence and removed final ى to make it a command
This is a preposition that shows distance or separation from something
Added هُمْ which means 'them' (for a group of males). Together with فَتَوَلَّ, this means 'so turn away from them'
This is a particle that shows the end point of something. As a particle, it has no root.
Added vowel marks and shadda for proper pronunciation
Added alif maqsura with superscript alif at the end. This particle connects to the word حِينٍ (time) to show 'until a time'.
The kasra (ِ) vowel mark helps make this a noun meaning 'time'
Added tanwin to show this is indefinite, meaning 'a' time rather than 'the' time
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to a command by adding أ at the start and changing the vowels - tells someone to see or look at something
Added و with fatha to connect this command to what came before it

steps.m: and watch them! (connected to فَسَوْفَ يُبْصِرُونَ - then they will see)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show who is being watched. This command is connected to the next phrase 'then they will see' showing a cause and effect relationship.
This is a particle that indicates something will happen in the future. The vowel marks help with pronunciation.

steps.m: then will/shall (connected to يُبْصِرُونَ - they will see)
steps.n: Added فَ (fa) at the beginning to show this happens as a result of what came before. This connects to the next word يُبْصِرُونَ (they will see), making the complete phrase 'then they will see'

steps.m: to see or understand with eyes or mind

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added ونَ to show multiple people doing the action
Added vowels to connect the root letters together to make a past tense verb
Changes from 'to punish' (action) into 'punishment' (thing/result)
Adds نا (our) to عذاب (punishment) to show it's Allah's punishment. Like saying 'my book' but here it's 'our punishment'
Adds 'with/by' to show how something is done
Adds فَ to connect this phrase to what came before it, showing sequence or result

steps.m: is it by our punishment that (they hasten)?
steps.n: Adds أَ to make this a question. This word connects to يَسْتَعْجِلُونَ (they hasten) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Shows someone is trying to make something happen faster
Shows the action is happening now or in the future

steps.m: they seek to hurry (referring to the disbelievers asking for Allah's punishment to come quickly)
steps.n: The ون ending is added to show multiple people (they) instead of one person (he). This connects to أَفَبِعَذَابِنَا (Then is it Our punishment) showing how the disbelievers were trying to rush Allah's punishment
This is a time word that shows when something happens. Here it connects to the word نَزَلَ (it descended) to show when that action happens.

steps.m: then when (connects to نَزَلَ - it descended)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens as a result of something before it, like saying 'and then when' or 'so when' in English
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word connects to بِسَاحَتِهِمْ (their courtyard) to show where he descended.
Connected the root letters with vowels. The middle weak letter و changes to ا.
Makes a feminine noun meaning 'open space' or 'courtyard'
Changes the word to show possession/connection
Adds third person masculine plural possessive suffix meaning 'their'
Adds preposition بِ meaning 'in/at'
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs

steps.m: then it became evil (connects to نَزَلَ - descended)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happens after something else. In this ayah, it shows what happens after 'it descended upon their territory'
The ا makes the word stronger, emphasizing how evil it was
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'morning' and added the ُ (damma) to show it's doing the action in سَآءَ (became evil)
Added مُ at the beginning to show this is about the person receiving the warning, not the one giving it. The َ (fatha) after ذ shows this is happening to them.

steps.m: warned one (ready to connect to plural)
steps.n: Added ِ (kasra) at the end to prepare for connecting the plural ending
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about many people who were warned

steps.m: the warned ones (referring to specific people who were warned)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ (al) at the beginning to point to specific warned people, and َ (fatha) at the end because this word is affected by something else in the sentence

steps.m: to turn, to be in charge of something

steps.n: Basic past tense pattern (fatha vowels) added to make 'he' form of verb meaning 'took charge'
Added shadda to show intensity and dropped the weak letter ي
Adding ت makes the action reflexive - doing to oneself
Added و at beginning to connect with previous statement. This command is connected to عَنْهُمْ which shows who to turn away from.
This is a preposition that shows distance or turning away from something

steps.m: away from them (object of وَتَوَلَّ - turn away)
steps.n: Adding هم at the end means 'them' (for a group of males). This word completes the meaning of وَتَوَلَّ (turn away) by showing who to turn away from.
Added basic vowel marks to show pronunciation
The letter ت gets doubled (shown by the shadda mark) to make the word mean 'until'
Added alif maqsura (ىٰ) at the end, which is characteristic of this particle. This word connects to حِينٍ (a time) in the ayah to show 'until a time'
The kasra (ِ) vowel mark helps make this a noun meaning 'time'

steps.m: a time/period (used with حَتَّىٰ to mean 'until a time')
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show this is indefinite. When used with حَتَّىٰ (until) before it, it means 'until a time'

steps.m: to see or perceive with eyes

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أ at the start makes it mean 'to cause someone to see/show'. The middle letter gets a sukun.
Adding sukun at the end and changing middle vowel makes it a command to one person
Added و with fatha at the start to mean 'and'
This is a particle that indicates something will happen in the future

steps.m: then will (connects to يُبْصِرُونَ - they will see)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens as a result or sequence. This connects to the next word يُبْصِرُونَ (they will see), showing they will definitely see in the future as a consequence.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Added يُ at start and changed vowels to show present tense
Added ونَ to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: to be free from any flaw or error

steps.n: Changed from a root meaning 'to be perfect' into a noun meaning 'perfection' by adding vowels

steps.m: glorified, exalted above all imperfection (describes رَبِّكَ - your Lord)
steps.n: Added ٰنَ at the end to create an emphatic form showing intense glorification. This word is describing رَبِّكَ (your Lord) in this ayah.

steps.m: to nurture, care for, and have authority over
lord (one who nurtures and has complete authority)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows intensity, indicating complete lordship and authority
Adding a kasra (small diagonal line) under the last letter to show 'my lord' instead of just 'lord'
Adding كَ shows this is your Lord. This word follows سُبْحٰنَ (glorified is) in the ayah.
Adding fatha and doubling the middle letter creates a noun meaning 'one who nurtures/develops'
Adding kasra at the end makes it into a form of address/calling out to one's Lord
Changed vowels and added shadda to create abstract noun
Forms a concrete noun from the abstract quality
Shows this word is possessed by رَبِّ (Lord) in the phrase 'Lord of might'
Adds the definite article to specify 'the' might/honor
Starting with the two separate words that will combine. عَنْ is a preposition meaning 'from/about', and مَا means 'what'

steps.m: from what/about what (describing يَصِفُونَ - what they describe)
steps.n: When عَنْ combines with مَا, the ن changes to م and gets a shadda (double mark). This is a common change in Arabic when certain letters come together.
When عَنْ combines with مَا, the ن assimilates into م with a shadda, and they become one word

steps.m: to describe something's qualities or attributes

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show past tense
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The و drops because it's a weak letter at the start.

steps.m: they describe (referring to those who attribute false things to Allah)
steps.n: Adding ون shows multiple people are doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to those who incorrectly describe or attribute things to Allah.

steps.m: to be at peace, to be safe and secure

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to be at peace' to the noun 'peace'
The word has tanween (double damma) at the end to show it means 'a peace' instead of just 'peace'

steps.m: and peace (connected to what came before)
steps.n: Adds 'and' to connect this statement of peace to the previous praise of Allah
This is a preposition that shows something is directed towards or resting upon something else. Here it connects 'peace' with 'the messengers' to mean 'peace upon the messengers'
Changed root 'send' into 'messengers' by adding مُ at start and ين at end to mean 'many messengers'

steps.m: the messengers (who are being greeted with peace in وَسَلٰمٌ عَلَى)
steps.n: Added ال at beginning to make it specific and َ at end to show it receives the greeting of peace
Added vowel marks to convert the root into a noun form
Adding ال makes the word definite, like adding 'the' in English
Adding وَ connects this word to what came before it. The اَلْ changes to ٱلْ after وَ.

steps.m: and the praise (subject of لِلَّهِ - belongs to Allah)
steps.n: The ُ shows this is the subject that belongs to Allah (لِلَّهِ) in this phrase
This is Allah's proper name, which doesn't come from a root. It's the base word we'll modify.
Added لِ at the beginning to show the meaning 'for' or 'to'. The first ا of الله is dropped when we add لِ.
Added ِ (kasra) at the end to show possession - that the praise belongs to Allah. This connects it to الْحَمْدُ (the praise) in the ayah.
Doubling of middle letter ب shows this is someone who nurtures and takes care of others completely
Adding kasra at the end shows this word owns or possesses what comes after it. Here it shows Allah is Lord of the worlds.
Forms a noun meaning 'that which is known' - used to refer to the entire created world/universe
Adds sound masculine plural ending to create plural form meaning 'worlds'
Adds definite article and shows this word is possessed by رَبِّ (Lord) in the phrase 'Lord of the worlds'
